vBRIEFInfo.author: panopticon-cli/0.0.0
vBRIEFInfo.description: Plan for PAN-1122: Trim OpenAI model catalog to 5 supported models

plan.title: Trim OpenAI model catalog to 5 supported models
plan.author: agent:claude-opus-4-7

narratives.Problem: The OpenAI model dropdown and backend allowed-models list contain 12 entries — a mix of legacy chat models (gpt-4o, gpt-4o-mini), reasoning models out of scope for standard chat (o3, o4-mini), speculative IDs that never shipped (gpt-5.5-mini, gpt-5.5-nano), and redundant flagships (gpt-5.4-pro vs gpt-5.5-pro). Users see noise; the backend accepts requests for IDs that may not route correctly. The OpenAIModel union type carries the same legacy entries.
narratives.Proposal: Settle on exactly five supported OpenAI models — gpt-5.4-nano (cheap), gpt-5.4-mini (fast), gpt-5.4 (balanced), gpt-5.5 (best), gpt-5.5-pro (max). Trim the frontend dropdown, the backend router allowed-models lists, the OpenAIModel TypeScript union, and stale references in cost/priority/alias tables. Add graceful migration redirects so existing user configs that pin a dropped ID resolve to gpt-5.4 (the balanced default) with a warning rather than failing.

autoDecisions.summary: gpt-5.4-nano is added to the backend router allowed-models list (currently absent there, present in frontend catalog).
autoDecisions.rationale: Issue lists it as the cheap-slot supported model; missing from backend would mean dropdown offers it but backend rejects it.

autoDecisions.summary: All dropped IDs (gpt-5.5-mini, gpt-5.5-nano, gpt-5.4-pro, o3, o4-mini, gpt-4o, gpt-4o-mini, o3-deep-research, gpt-5.3-codex, gpt-5.2) redirect to gpt-5.4 via the existing LEGACY_MODEL_REDIRECTS mechanism in src/lib/model-capabilities.ts.
autoDecisions.rationale: Issue acceptance criterion says configs should fall back gracefully to gpt-5.4. The redirect map already exists for two of these IDs; extending it is the lowest-risk migration path and emits the standard warning.

autoDecisions.summary: OpenAIModel union type is narrowed to the five supported IDs only — dropped IDs are removed from the type, not retained as deprecated aliases.
autoDecisions.rationale: Migration is handled at the redirect layer; keeping dropped IDs in the type would defeat the trim and let new code pin models we no longer support.

autoDecisions.summary: Inspector backing-model display labels for gpt-4o and gpt-5.4-pro are removed alongside the type narrowing.
autoDecisions.rationale: These labels become dead code once the type rejects the IDs.
It is a prompt asset describing historical OpenAI lineup for a research agent, not a runtime catalog. Out of scope for this trim.

items.title: Narrow OpenAIModel type to the five supported IDs

narrative.Action: Edit src/lib/settings.ts: replace the OpenAIModel union with exactly 'gpt-5.4-nano' | 'gpt-5.4-mini' | 'gpt-5.4' | 'gpt-5.5' | 'gpt-5.5-pro'. Also update the OpenAI default-models array at settings.ts:223 to the same five IDs. This is the type foundation other beads encode against.

subItems.title: OpenAIModel type lists exactly the five supported IDs and no others
settings.ts:223 default OpenAI models array matches the five supported IDs
npm run typecheck passes (surfacing every downstream reference that needs updating)

items.title: Add legacy redirects so dropped IDs fall back to gpt-5.4

narrative.Action: Extend LEGACY_MODEL_REDIRECTS in src/lib/model-capabilities.ts so every dropped OpenAI ID (gpt-5.5-mini, gpt-5.5-nano, gpt-5.4-pro, o3, o4-mini, gpt-4o, gpt-4o-mini, o3-deep-research, gpt-5.3-codex, gpt-5.2) maps to 'gpt-5.4'. The redirect emits the existing warning when a saved config references a dropped ID.

subItems.title: All ten dropped OpenAI IDs are present in LEGACY_MODEL_REDIRECTS and map to gpt-5.4
Loading a user config that references a dropped ID resolves to gpt-5.4 and logs the existing redirect warning rather than erroring

items.title: Sync backend router allowed-models to the five supported IDs

narrative.Action: Update the OpenAI `models` arrays at src/lib/router-config.ts:67 and :134, and src/lib/providers.ts:81 and :180, to exactly ['gpt-5.4-nano', 'gpt-5.4-mini', 'gpt-5.4', 'gpt-5.5', 'gpt-5.5-pro']. Also update the OpenAI include-check at providers.ts:180 so that any model ID outside the supported set falls through to the unknown-provider error path.

subItems.title: All four backend allowed-models sites list exactly the five supported IDs
Requesting a dropped OpenAI ID through the router (bypassing the redirect layer) produces a clear error naming the unsupported model

items.title: Trim frontend OpenAI dropdown to the five supported models

narrative.Action: Edit src/dashboard/frontend/src/components/Settings/modelCatalog.ts: remove gpt-5.5-mini, gpt-5.5-nano, gpt-5.4-pro, o3, o4-mini, gpt-4o, gpt-4o-mini from MODELS_BY_PROVIDER.openai. The remaining five entries (gpt-5.5-pro, gpt-5.5, gpt-5.4, gpt-5.4-mini, gpt-5.4-nano) keep their existing tier/cost/capability metadata.

subItems.title: The Settings OpenAI provider dropdown shows exactly five models when rendered

items.title: Remove stale references to dropped OpenAI IDs across cost, priority, and alias tables

narrative.Action: Remove dropped-ID entries from: src/lib/cost.ts (gpt-5.4-pro pricing row); src/lib/settings-api.ts (priority map at :718 and :722 — drop gpt-5.4-pro, gpt-4o, gpt-4o-mini); src/dashboard/server/routes/settings.ts (apiModel alias map — drop gpt-5.4-pro, o3-deep-research, gpt-4o, gpt-4o-mini, o1); src/dashboard/frontend/src/components/inspector/utils.ts (backing-model labels at :121, :129); src/lib/model-fallback.ts (dropped-ID entries in PROVIDER_HINTS, CROSS_PROVIDER_FALLBACK, MODEL_PRIORITY); src/lib/model-capabilities.ts (dedicated gpt-5.4-pro capabilities entry at :328). The CLIProxy registry alias in src/lib/agents.ts (gpt-5.4-pro → gpt-5.4) can be removed since the redirect layer now covers it. Add a gpt-5.4-nano capabilities entry if missing for parity with the other four supported IDs.

subItems.title: No source file under src/ references dropped OpenAI IDs except via the LEGACY_MODEL_REDIRECTS map
gpt-5.4-nano has a capabilities entry in src/lib/model-capabilities.ts
npm run typecheck and npm run lint both pass
npm test passes (catches any test snapshot/fixture that referenced a dropped ID)
